key_insight: The paper extends real-time TDDFT with first-principles electron-phonon scattering so laser-excited carriers can relax, decohere, and redistribute in energy and momentum.
engineering_relevance: This provides a predictive simulation tool for nonequilibrium carrier dynamics in crystalline materials, useful for interpreting ultrafast spectroscopy and designing optoelectronic devices.
method: Propagate the reduced one-body density matrix in real time within rt-TDDFT.
Add phonon-mediated collision integrals from electron-phonon self-energies using the Born-Markov approximation.
Retain coherent electronic evolution while enabling irreversible scattering and relaxation.
evidence_signal: The abstract presents a theoretical framework and claims its ability to capture relaxation and time-resolved spectroscopic signatures, but it does not report benchmark datasets or numerical validation here.
watch_item: The Born-Markov treatment may limit accuracy when memory effects, strong coupling, or highly non-Markovian phonon dynamics are important.
paper_title: First-principles electron-phonon scattering in real-time TDDFT
